items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: This critical vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to achieve remote code execution by supplying a crafted serialized PHP object in the CacheWarmer cookie. It affects all Mirasvit Cache Warmer versions before 1.11.12.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass. Adobe is not aware of any exploits in the wild for these issues.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This flaw, part of APSB26-49, allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access to the affected application. Exploitation requires no user interaction and can be performed over the network.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This security update addresses multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read. Adobe is not aware of any exploits in the wild for these issues.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This high-severity vulnerability, addressed in APSB26-05, allows a low-privileged attacker to bypass security measures and gain unauthorized view access to data. It is classified as an Incorrect Authorization issue (CWE-863).

items.title: Privilege Escalation Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This high-severity vulnerability, resolved in APSB26-05, could allow an attacker to gain escalated privileges within the system. It is one of several privilege escalation issues addressed in the bulletin.

items.title: Privilege Escalation Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Addressed in APSB26-05, this high-severity flaw enables an attacker to achieve privilege escalation. This is part of a set of critical and important vulnerabilities patched in the March 2026 update.

items.title: Privilege Escalation Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This high-severity privilege escalation vulnerability was fixed as part of the APSB26-05 security update. Successful exploitation could allow an attacker to gain higher access rights.

items.title: Privilege Escalation Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Included in the APSB26-05 bulletin, this high-severity vulnerability allows for privilege escalation. Adobe recommends applying the security patches promptly to mitigate potential attacks.

items.title: Privilege Escalation Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This high-severity vulnerability, patched in APSB26-05, could lead to an attacker gaining elevated privileges. It is one of several critical issues addressed in the March 2026 security update.

items.title: Security Feature Bypass Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This high-severity vulnerability, part of APSB26-05, allows an attacker to bypass security features. It contributes to the overall risk of compromise if not patched.
